articles.title: This Is the Best Way to Pack a Beach Bag So You Can Fit Everything—And Actually Find What You’re Looking For
articles.content:
作为一个热爱海滩但皮肤白皙的人，我甚至在踏上沙滩之前就需要涂满防晒霜、穿上防晒服，所以我绝对是个“挑剔”的海滩游客。我喜欢带上海滩帽子、冲浪服、四种不同的防晒霜（这很有必要，相信我）、发夹、Kindle阅读器、一壶冰水、一些零食、一条毛巾，还有回家时要换的干衣服。这还只是我自己的——更别提还得为两个青春期前期的孩子带的那些装备了！

当你的海滩包里要装这么多东西时，你就得在收纳上讲究策略，否则你就会花上一整天在包里翻找，或者疯狂地把所有东西倒在沙子上，就为了找那支SPF润唇膏。“一个杂乱无章的海滩包通常会引发连锁反应，”Beaches Turks & Caicos的管家经理露西·普菲努如是说。“你的防晒霜因为没有密封好而泄漏，湿泳衣把书或罩衫都浸透了，沙子钻进所有东西里，然后你不得不把整个包倒空，就为了找房卡或手机。”是的，我也有过这种经历！

幸运的是，还有更好的方法。请继续阅读普菲努关于最佳海滩包收纳方式的建议，包括她看到客人最常犯的错误——以及每个海滩游客都需要但通常会忘记的那一样必备物品。

订阅《读者文摘》的Read Up通讯，获取更多整理技巧、幽默故事、清洁妙招、旅行建议、科技资讯和有趣事实，整个星期都有。

“最大的错误之一就是我看到客人们觉得需要为一天的海滩之行打包所有东西，”普菲努说。“许多客人带着塞满'以防万一'物品的海滩包到达，结果发现自己包里有一半东西几乎没用过。”

避免过度打包的陷阱在几个方面让生活更轻松。正如普菲努所指出的：“轻装上阵不仅让你的包更容易携带，也更容易找到你需要的东西，这样你就能放松身心，享受海滩了。”（好吧，也许我应该重新考虑带四种防晒霜的决定。）

另一个常见错误？没有组织策略地把东西往包里一扔。“用系统化的方式打包并创建分区，会让海滩之行的其余部分更加愉快和轻松，”普菲努说。“当每样东西都有指定位置时，你花在看东西上的时间就会减少，花在创造回忆上的时间就会增多。”

决定你一天成败的不仅仅是包里装了什么——还有你如何打包。以下是专家认可的打包海滩包并保持冷静的方法。

在尝试了多年的潮流海滩包之后，我偏爱有EVA泡沫底部的包，比如Yeti Carryall Tote或Original Bogg Bag。这些坚固、有型的包不会在沙子上倒塌，防水且易于冲洗清洁，泡沫底部还能在您的物品和沙子之间增加一层保护。

Yeti包有拉链口袋、分隔层和外部挂环，您可以在这里挂上防晒霜或其他经常需要拿取的东西。Original Bogg Bag配有可拆卸的透明拉链小袋，您可以购买似乎无穷无尽的配件，如分隔板、杯架和桌面托盘来定制您的包。

这类包比帆布或布质手提袋重，但交换条件是它们更容易整理、防水，而且在一天结束时清洁起来也很方便。

“在Beaches，我们总是提前几步思考，确保在客人意识到自己需要之前，一切都已精心放置、便于取用，”普菲努说。这种战略性思维对您的海滩包同样适用，因为您应该为各种物品创建分区。

她建议，首先放入包底部的东西应该是您不会马上需要的东西，比如一天结束时要换的干衣服或额外的罩衫。“中间区域是放整天会用到的物品，如书籍或其他个人用品，最上层区域应该留给海滩必需品，如防晒霜、太阳镜和帽子，这样它们始终触手可及。”

至于沙滩巾，把它纵向卷起来，竖着放在包的一侧。这样您就可以方便地拿取，同时又紧凑地收纳好了。

“我总是鼓励客人给贵重物品指定一个位置，比如手机、钱包和钥匙，”普菲努说。“这是一个小习惯，但可以节省时间，保护您的物品免受沙子和水的侵害，让您专注于享受海滩，而不是寻找必需品。”

电子书阅读器和任何其他小型电子产品（如耳机或便携式音箱）也是如此，这样它们就不会被水损坏。我也喜欢把小件个人物品（如发夹和润唇膏）放在拉链小袋里，这样它们就不会丢失在包底。

任何需要冷藏的东西都应该放在专门的冷却袋里，但如果您带的是零食，如袋装椒盐脆饼或薯片，请把它们放在靠近顶部的地方，这样就不会被压碎。我喜欢把我的可重复使用的水瓶放在单独的口袋里（如果我的包有的话），如果没有就放在底部，因为它比较重。

您需要把防晒霜放在上面，可能放在拉链口袋或小袋里，这样一整天都方便随时取用。“太阳镜应该存放在保护盒里以防止划伤，”普菲努说。如果您带的是有型的防晒帽，它应该放在所有东西的上面，这样就不会被压坏。“太阳帽很容易变形，所以给它自己的空间很重要，”普菲努说。

我还想给其他家长一个建议：把泳镜放在手边，这样您的孩子就不会把您海滩包里的所有东西都扔到沙子上，漫无目的地翻找了。

无论您的孩子是喜欢用铲子和水桶在沙子里挖东西的年纪，还是已经大到想玩接球或飞盘的年纪，一个单独的网袋是最佳选择。“这有助于将沙子隔离在您的其他物品之外，”普菲努说。而且，用网袋，您可以先把玩具冲洗干净再放回后备箱或家里或酒店房间，沙子和水也不会积聚在您的主要海滩包里。

不知怎么的，在海滩待了一天之后，不只是我们的头发变大了，我们的东西似乎也变多了。（或者也许是因为维生素D和海风让我们变懒了！）此外，几乎所有东西都覆盖着一层沙子。这就是为什么在一天结束时正确地重新打包您的海滩包，和您早晨的打包环节一样重要——而且您甚至可以在拆包之前就为成功做好准备。

“沙子是海滩度假乐趣的一部分，但几个简单的习惯可以帮助防止它侵占您的海滩包，”普菲努说。她建议把包放在海滩椅或毛巾上，而不是直接放在沙子上，这样沙子就会少粘一些。您还应该尽量把包放在阴凉处，以防止电子产品过热、冰水快速变温，以及润唇膏和其他化妆品融化。

首先，确保您在顺风处，而且离其他海滩游客足够远，不会造成小型沙尘暴，然后用力抖几下您的毛巾。卷起来而不是叠起来，有助于将剩余的沙子控制在内部。然后把您的毛巾放回包底作为第一层。

“我经常看到客人把湿泳衣或沾满沙子的鞋子直接扔回海滩包，结果发现其他东西都湿了或覆盖着沙子，”普菲努说，她建议携带防水袋或湿物袋来避免这个问题。

如果有淋浴区，用水冲洗人字拖或其他防水凉鞋上的沙子。“这个简单的习惯可以保持其他物品的清洁、干燥，并为接下来的一切做好准备，无论您是去吃午餐、开车、去泳池还是回酒店房间，”她说。
articles.summary:
As someone who loves the beach but is so fair-skinned that I need to bathe in sunscreen and layer on the sun protective clothing before I even set foot on the sand, I’m as high-maintenance a beachgoer as they come. I like to have a beach hat, rash guard, four different types of sunblock (it’s necessary, trust me), hair clips, my Kindle, a thermos of ice water, a snack or two, a towel and dry clothes to change into at the end of the day. And that’s just for me—never mind the gear I have to lug along for my two tweens!

When you’re packing this much in your beach bag, you need to be strategic about what goes where or you’ll spend your day digging through it or frantically tossing everything in the sand to find that one SPF lip balm you’re searching for. “A disorganized beach bag usually creates a chain reaction,” says Lucy Pfinu, the butler manager at Beaches Turks & Caicos. “Your sunscreen leaks because it isn’t sealed, your wet swimsuit ends up soaking your book or cover-up, sand gets into everything, and suddenly you’re emptying your entire bag just to find your room key or phone.” Yep, been there!

Fortunately, there is a better way. Read on for Pfinu’s tips on the best way to pack your beach bag, including the most common mistakes she sees guests make—and the one must-have every beachgoer needs but usually forgets.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more organizing tips, humor, cleaning, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

What is the most common mistake people make with their beach bags?

“One of the biggest mistakes I see guests make is feeling like they need to pack everything for a day at the beach,” Pfinu says. “Many guests arrive with beach bags full of ‘just in case’ items, only to realize they hardly use half of what’s inside.”

And avoiding the trap of overpacking makes life easier in a few ways. As Pfinu notes, “Packing lighter not only makes your bag easier to carry, but it also makes it much easier to find what you need so you can relax and enjoy the beach.” (OK, maybe I should rethink bringing four different types of sunscreen.)

Another common mistake? Tossing things into your bag without any organizational strategy. “Packing with a system and creating zones will make the rest of the beach day much more enjoyable and effortless,” Pfinu says. “When everything has a designated spot, you’ll spend less time searching and more time making memories.”

What’s the best way to pack a beach bag?

It’s not only what goes in your beach bag that can make or break your day—it’s how you pack it too. Here’s the expert-approved method for packing that beach bag and keeping your sanity.

1. Start with the right bag

After trying tons of trendy beach bags over the years, I’m partial to bags with an EVA foam bottom, such as the Yeti Carryall Tote or an Original Bogg Bag. These sturdy, structured bags won’t flop over on the sand, are waterproof and easy to rinse clean, and the foam bottom adds a layer of protection between your belongings and the sand.

Yeti bags have zippered pockets, dividers and exterior loops where you can hook on sunblock or other items you know you reach for regularly. The Original Bogg Bag comes with two removable, clear zippered pouches, and you can buy seemingly endless accessories like dividers, cup holders and a tray table top to customize your bag.

These styles of bags are heavier than a canvas or cloth tote bag, but the trade-off is that they’re easier to organize, waterproof and a breeze to clean at the end of the day.

2. Layer strategically

“At Beaches, we’re always thinking a few steps ahead, making sure everything is thoughtfully placed and easy to access before guests even realize they need it,” Pfinu says. This strategic thinking works well for your beach bag, since you should create zones for your various items.

The first items to go in the bottom of your bag should be the things you won’t need right away, she says, such as dry clothing for the end of the day or an extra cover-up. “The middle zone is for things you’ll use throughout the day, like a book or other personal items, and the top zone should be reserved for your beach-day essentials, like sunscreen, sunglasses and a hat, so they’re always within easy reach.”

As for a beach towel, roll it up lengthwise, and stand it upright on one side of your bag. That way, you have easy access to it, but it’s also packed compactly.

3. Store important items in waterproof pouches or zippered pockets

“I always encourage guests to have a designated place for their valuables, such as phone, wallet and keys,” Pfinu says. “It’s a small habit, but it saves time, protects your belongings from sand and water, and lets you focus on enjoying the beach instead of searching for your essentials.”

The same goes for your e-reader and any other small electronics like earbuds or portable speakers, so they don’t get damaged by water. I also like to store smaller personal items like hair clips and lip balm in a zippered pouch so they don’t get lost at the bottom of my bag.

4. Separate the snacks

Anything that needs to be refrigerated should go in a dedicated cooler, but if you’re bringing snacks like bagged pretzels or chips, store these near the top so they don’t get squished. I like to keep my reusable water bottle in a separate pocket, if my bag has one, or at the bottom if it doesn’t, since it’s heavy.

5. Pack your most-reached-for items last

You’ll want to keep sunscreen on top, potentially in a zippered pocket or pouch, so it’s easy to grab throughout the day. “Sunglasses should be stored in a protective case to prevent scratches,” Pfinu says. And if you’re bringing a structured sun hat, it should rest on top of everything else so it doesn’t get crushed. “A sun hat can easily lose its shape, so giving it its own space is important,” Pfinu says.

I would also suggest to fellow parents that you keep the goggles handy so your kids don’t start throwing all the contents of your beach bag onto the sand as they hunt for them willy-nilly.

6. Corral the kids’ toys

Whether your kids are at an age where they still enjoy digging in the sand with a shovel and bucket or they’re old enough to want to play catch or toss a frisbee, a separate net bag is the way to go. “This helps keep sand contained away from your other belongings,” Pfinu says. Also, with a net bag, you can rinse the toys before putting them back in your trunk or in your home or hotel room, and the sand and water won’t pool in your primary beach bag.

How do you repack all this so it fits—and you don’t end up with a sandy mess?

Somehow, it’s not just our hair that gets bigger after a day at a beach—our belongings seem to multiply too. (Or maybe all that vitamin D and sea air just make us lazy!) Plus, nearly everything is coated in a layer of sand. That’s why repacking your beach bag the right way at day’s end is just as important as your morning packing session—and you can set yourself up for success before you even unpack.

Keep your beach bag off the sand and in the shade

“Sand is part of the fun of a beach vacation, but a few simple habits can help keep it from taking over your beach bag,” Pfinu says. She recommends keeping your bag on a beach chair or towel rather than directly on the sand, so it picks up less of the gritty stuff. You should also aim to keep your bag in the shade to prevent electronics from overheating, ice water from warming up quickly, and lip balms and other makeup from melting.

Shake off as much sand as possible

First, make sure you’re downwind and far enough away from any other beachgoers that you won’t create a mini sandstorm, then give your towel a few firm shakes. Rolling it, rather than folding it, will help keep any remaining sand contained within. Then place your towel(s) back in the bottom of your bag as the first layer.

Place wet items in waterproof bags

“I often see guests toss a wet swimsuit or sandy shoes back into their beach bag, only to find everything else damp or covered in sand,” says Pfinu, who suggests bringing a waterproof pouch or wet bag to avoid this issue.

If there’s a shower area, rinse sand off flip-flops or other waterproof sandals. “This simple habit keeps the rest of your belongings clean, dry and ready for whatever comes next, whether you’re heading to lunch, the car, the pool or back to your hotel room,” she says.

What else should you keep in mind about your beach bag?

  • Toss garbage as soon as possible. Beaches can get windy, and you don’t want your chip bag flying away into the ocean or lake.
  • Look around for your belongings to make sure you have everything. Yes, this should go without saying, but I can’t tell you how many pairs of goggles my kids have lost between the two of them. Here’s a handy three-second trick to ensure you don’t forget anything.
  • Unpack your beach bag as soon as you get home, ideally outdoors. Of course, this will help you track less sand inside your house. Shake out your towels, line-dry your suits and give waterproof bags a quick hose-off.

Source:

  • Lucy Pfinu, butler manager at Beaches Turks & Caicos; interviewed, July 2026
